actions.description: Adds a comment to an existing ticket.

properties.description: API URL of the ticket resource.
properties.name: url

properties.name: from

properties.name: to

properties.name: created_at

properties.name: generated_timestamp

properties.name: type

properties.name: priority

properties.name: status

properties.name: has_incidents

properties.name: is_public

properties.description: Automatically assigned when the user is created
properties.name: id

properties.description: The user's API url
properties.name: url

properties.description: The user's name
properties.name: name

properties.description: The user's primary email address. *Writeable on create only. On update, a secondary email is added. See Email Address
properties.name: email

properties.description: The time the user was created
properties.name: created_at

properties.description: The time the user was last updated
properties.name: updated_at

properties.description: The user's time zone. See Time Zone
properties.name: time_zone

properties.description: The time zone for the user
properties.name: iana_time_zone

properties.description: The user's primary phone number. See Phone Number below
properties.name: phone

properties.description: Whether the phone number is shared or not. See Phone Number below
properties.name: shared_phone_number

properties.description: The user's profile picture represented as an Attachment object
properties.name: photo

properties.description: The user's language identifier
properties.name: locale_id

properties.description: The user's locale. A BCP-47 compliant tag for the locale. If both "locale" and "locale_id" are present on create or update, "locale_id" is ignored and only "locale" is used.
properties.name: locale

properties.description: The id of the user's organization. If the user has more than one organization memberships, the id of the user's default organization. If updating, see Organization ID
properties.name: organization_id

properties.description: The user's role. Possible values are "end-user", "agent", or "admin"
properties.name: role

properties.description: Any of the user's identities is verified. See User Identities
properties.name: verified

properties.description: Last time the user was active.
properties.name: last_active

properties.description: A unique identifier from another system. The API treats the id as case insensitive. Example: "ian1" and "IAN1" are the same value.
properties.name: external_id

properties.description: The user's tags. Only present if your account has user tagging enabled
properties.name: tags

properties.description: An alias displayed to end users
properties.name: alias

properties.description: false if the user has been deleted
properties.name: active

properties.description: If the user is shared from a different Zendesk Support instance. Shared users can be added to organizations but cannot be modified through update requests. Any attempt to update a shared user results in a 403 Forbidden error. Ticket sharing accounts only
properties.name: shared

properties.description: If the user is a shared agent from a different Zendesk Support instance. Ticket sharing accounts only
properties.name: shared_agent

properties.description: Last time the user signed in to Zendesk Support or made an API request using an API token
properties.name: last_login_at

properties.description: If two factor authentication is enabled
properties.name: two_factor_auth_enabled

properties.description: The user's signature. Only agents and admins can have signatures
properties.name: signature

properties.description: Any details you want to store about the user, such as an address
properties.name: details

properties.description: Any notes you want to store about the user
properties.name: notes

properties.description: The user's role id. 0 for a custom agent, 1 for a light agent, 2 for a chat agent, 3 for a chat agent added to the Support account as a contributor (Chat Phase 4), 4 for an admin, and 5 for a billing admin
properties.name: role_type

properties.description: A custom role if the user is an agent on the Enterprise plan or above
properties.name: custom_role_id

properties.description: Whether the user is a billing admin.
properties.name: is_billing_admin

properties.description: Designates whether the user has forum moderation capabilities
properties.name: moderator

properties.description: Specifies which tickets the user has access to. Possible values are: "organization", "groups", "assigned", "requested", null. "groups" and "assigned" are valid only for agents. If you pass an invalid value to an end user (for example, "groups"), they will be assigned to "requested", regardless of their previous access
properties.name: ticket_restriction

properties.description: true if the user can only create private comments
properties.name: only_private_comments

properties.description: If the agent has any restrictions; false for admins and unrestricted agents, true for other agents
properties.name: restricted_agent

properties.description: If the agent is suspended. Tickets from suspended users are also suspended, and these users cannot sign in to the end user portal
properties.name: suspended

properties.description: The id of the user's default group
properties.name: default_group_id

properties.description: This parameter is inert and has no effect. It may be deprecated in the future. Previously, this parameter determined whether a user could access a CSV report in a legacy Guide dashboard. This dashboard has been removed. See Announcing Guide legacy reporting upgrade to Explore
properties.name: report_csv

properties.description: Values of custom fields in the user's profile. See User Fields
properties.name: user_fields

properties.description: Channel-level suspension state for the user. The value is null if the user has no active channel-level suspension
properties.name: suspension_details

properties.description: Automatically assigned when the user is created
properties.name: id

properties.description: The user's API url
properties.name: url

properties.description: The user's name
properties.name: name

properties.description: The user's primary email address. *Writeable on create only. On update, a secondary email is added. See Email Address
properties.name: email

properties.description: The time the user was created
properties.name: created_at

properties.description: The time the user was last updated
properties.name: updated_at

properties.description: The user's time zone. See Time Zone
properties.name: time_zone

properties.description: The time zone for the user
properties.name: iana_time_zone

properties.description: The user's primary phone number. See Phone Number below
properties.name: phone

properties.description: Whether the phone number is shared or not. See Phone Number below
properties.name: shared_phone_number

properties.description: The user's profile picture represented as an Attachment object
properties.name: photo

properties.description: The user's language identifier
properties.name: locale_id

properties.description: The user's locale. A BCP-47 compliant tag for the locale. If both "locale" and "locale_id" are present on create or update, "locale_id" is ignored and only "locale" is used.
properties.name: locale

properties.description: The id of the user's organization. If the user has more than one organization memberships, the id of the user's default organization. If updating, see Organization ID
properties.name: organization_id

properties.description: The user's role. Possible values are "end-user", "agent", or "admin"
properties.name: role

properties.description: Any of the user's identities is verified. See User Identities
properties.name: verified

properties.description: Last time the user was active.
properties.name: last_active

properties.description: A unique identifier from another system. The API treats the id as case insensitive. Example: "ian1" and "IAN1" are the same value.
properties.name: external_id

properties.description: The user's tags. Only present if your account has user tagging enabled
properties.name: tags

properties.description: An alias displayed to end users
properties.name: alias

properties.description: false if the user has been deleted
properties.name: active

properties.description: If the user is shared from a different Zendesk Support instance. Shared users can be added to organizations but cannot be modified through update requests. Any attempt to update a shared user results in a 403 Forbidden error. Ticket sharing accounts only
properties.name: shared

properties.description: If the user is a shared agent from a different Zendesk Support instance. Ticket sharing accounts only
properties.name: shared_agent

properties.description: Last time the user signed in to Zendesk Support or made an API request using an API token
properties.name: last_login_at

properties.description: If two factor authentication is enabled
properties.name: two_factor_auth_enabled

properties.description: The user's signature. Only agents and admins can have signatures
properties.name: signature

properties.description: Any details you want to store about the user, such as an address
properties.name: details

properties.description: Any notes you want to store about the user
properties.name: notes

properties.description: The user's role id. 0 for a custom agent, 1 for a light agent, 2 for a chat agent, 3 for a chat agent added to the Support account as a contributor (Chat Phase 4), 4 for an admin, and 5 for a billing admin
properties.name: role_type

properties.description: A custom role if the user is an agent on the Enterprise plan or above
properties.name: custom_role_id

properties.description: Whether the user is a billing admin.
properties.name: is_billing_admin

properties.description: Designates whether the user has forum moderation capabilities
properties.name: moderator

properties.description: Specifies which tickets the user has access to. Possible values are: "organization", "groups", "assigned", "requested", null. "groups" and "assigned" are valid only for agents. If you pass an invalid value to an end user (for example, "groups"), they will be assigned to "requested", regardless of their previous access
properties.name: ticket_restriction

properties.description: true if the user can only create private comments
properties.name: only_private_comments

properties.description: If the agent has any restrictions; false for admins and unrestricted agents, true for other agents
properties.name: restricted_agent

properties.description: If the agent is suspended. Tickets from suspended users are also suspended, and these users cannot sign in to the end user portal
properties.name: suspended

properties.description: The id of the user's default group
properties.name: default_group_id

properties.description: This parameter is inert and has no effect. It may be deprecated in the future. Previously, this parameter determined whether a user could access a CSV report in a legacy Guide dashboard. This dashboard has been removed. See Announcing Guide legacy reporting upgrade to Explore
properties.name: report_csv

properties.description: Values of custom fields in the user's profile. See User Fields
properties.name: user_fields

properties.description: Channel-level suspension state for the user. The value is null if the user has no active channel-level suspension
properties.name: suspension_details

properties.description: This parameter is inert and has no effect. It may be deprecated in the future. Previously, this parameter determined whether a user could access a CSV report in a legacy Guide dashboard. This dashboard has been removed. See Announcing Guide legacy reporting upgrade to Explore
properties.name: report_csv

properties.description: Values of custom fields in the user's profile. See User Fields
properties.name: user_fields

properties.description: Channel-level suspension state for the user. The value is null if the user has no active channel-level suspension
